measured_note: measured: Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct tokenizer (transformers==5.13.0), 3 payload sizes x 3 seeds each = 9 base64 samples ([1024, 4096, 16384] bytes, seeds [0, 1, 2]), 63744 tokens total for 516096 bits of original payload -> 8.0964 bits/token (1.3498 base64 chars/token, 1011.81 tokens/KB). Compare to the 6.0 bits/token analytic default (base64_bits_per_token()): HIGHER than 6.0 means the analytic estimate was favorable to heliogram's economic claim -- BPE merges commonly give base64 text MORE than 1 char/token, which pushes bits/token below the naive log2(64)=6 estimate.
